summary: Russian operators from the Rubikon Center reported 136 targets eliminated on March 28, including Ukrainian personnel and fixed-wing UAVs across the Donbas, Sumy, and Krasny Liman directions. Ukrainian FPV drones damaged Russian Gerbera drone launchers in Crimea, and a Ukrainian T-64 tank with non-standard armor was destroyed in Konstantinovka by FPV teams from Russia’s 4th OMSBr. Russian forces struck Ukrainian UAV control points with FAB-3000 and FAB-500 bombs equipped with UMPC glide kits, and used RBK-500 cluster munitions against positions near Maryevka and Shevchenko. The Russian "Yolka" anti-drone system intercepted a Ukrainian strike drone at over 1.5 km in a border region, detonating its payload. Ukraine’s 36th Marine Brigade survived an FPV attack near Kostiantinivka after detecting the drone with a detector. A Russian Ka-52 helicopter was downed near Nadiivka, with the crew killed during a rescue attempt by follow-up drones. Ukraine is mass-producing a ring-wing FPV drone design via the company "Ushkuynik," known for fiber-optic drones. Russian reconnaissance drones routinely deploy parachutes on command or after being hit, allowing recovery.
